title: Tupperware Drawer Organizer for Base Cabinets
description: Stop losing lids! Transform your kitchen storage with this beginner friendly guide to building a DIY wood Tupperware organizer inside a deep base cabinet.

materials.name: Stain/paint of choice - I used wood wax

steps.body: Cut the Tupperware sides to size using the Kreg track or circular saw. Round the top edge using a jigsaw. I used a bowl to get the rounded edge I wanted then cut to size with a jigsaw.
Cut the back to size. Set your pocket hole jig to 3/4" and drill pocket holes as shown in the picture.
Cut the front drawer piece to size. Drill pocket holes at 3/4" as shown in the picture.
Cut the bottom to size. Make sure you are using the 1/2" plywood for this.

Cut a groove on the inside bottom of each piece you just cut except for the drawer bottom. The groove should be 1/2" up from the bottom and it will be 1/2" wide and 1/4" deep. You can cut this with a track saw/circular saw or use a table saw. Make sure the bottom fits easily in those grooves.

I recommend sanding all the pieces now so you don't have too later!
Attach the back to the sides using wood glue and 1 1/4" pocket screws.
After you've slide the drawer bottom into the grooves, attach the front using wood glue and 1 1/4" pocket screws. Make sure the pocket holes face out as shown in the picture.
Cut the shelf to size and drill 3/4" pocket holes as shown in the picture.
Attach the shelf to the organizer using wood glue and 1 1/4" pocket screws. Make sure it is 8 1/4" down from the top of the organizer as shown in the photo. The pocket holes should be facing down.
Cut the shelf dividers to size. Round the front, top edge with a jigsaw. I recommend edge banding the front and top before installing these.
Screw the shelf dividers in as shown in the picture. Use 1 1/4" wood screw and wood glue. Make sure they are spaced evenly apart. They should be screwed in from the back and the bottom of the shelf.
Cut the drawer front t size. Using a jigsaw cut a handle into the front. The design is up to you!
Screw the drawer front onto the organizer from the backside of the drawer. Use 1 1/4" wood screws as shown in the picture.
Screw or nail on the bottom pieces of 1/4" plywood at the bottom so the slides can glide easily.
Iron on edge banding on all exposed plywood edges.
Stain and finish. I used a wood wax to finish it off that is kitchen friendly.

If your kitchen cabinet has a lip, screw a spacer block to the inside of the cabinet the same length as the drawer slides. I used 3/4" plywood.
Then screw in the drawer slides to the spacers, put the Tupperware drawer on top and pull out the slides. Screw the Tupperware drawer into the slides from the bottom. Make sure it moves nice and smooth and adjust if needed.
Put in your Tupperware and you've succesfully built yourself an organizer!
